title: Production Budget
event: Reyes Co. budgets sales of 24,000 units. It wants ending finished goods of 3,000 units and begins the period with 2,000 units. Compute required production.

computationPaths.narration: Produce enough to cover sales AND the inventory build — minus what's already on the shelf.

memorize.body: Production = budgeted sales + desired ENDING inventory − BEGINNING inventory.
Signs discipline: ending inventory ADDS (you must make it), beginning SUBTRACTS (it's already made). Flipping them is the classic miss.

questions.prompt: Sales 24,000 · desired ending FG 3,000 · beginning FG 2,000. Units to produce = ?

distractors.feedback: Inventory signs flipped. ADD the ending target, SUBTRACT the beginning stock: 25,000.
That ignores inventory entirely — the 1,000-unit build must also be produced.
